websitePages.name: What is FTC
websitePages.text:
-A Future Travel Credit (FTC) is a voucher/credit for the value of your ticket.

-The airline gives you this credit instead of points or cash refund when you choose to cancel a non-refundable ticket.

-This credit has a validity of 1 year from the issuance date can only be used  by same Passenger and same airline

websitePages.name: $25 Airline Allowance
websitePages.text:
You may get up to $25 back per round-trip award ticket for airline extras, as long as you used FlexPoints to book the ticket through the Rewards Center online or by phone.
 
To qualify:
- You must use at least 10,000 FlexPoints per round-trip ticket
- The extra purchase must be made on your travel dates
- The purchase must be charged to your eligible FlexPerks card
- The charge must be directly with the airline
 
Examples of covered purchases:
- Checked bag fees
- In-flight food or drinks
- Airline lounge membership
 
Please note:
- If you used Real-Time Rewards to pay for airfare, this benefit does not apply
- You need to call Cardmember Service within 90 days after travel to request the credit
- If approved, the credit will show on your account within 4 to 6 weeks
- Your account must be open and in good standing

websitePages.name: U.S. Bank car Rental Collision Damage Waiver "CDW"
websitePages.text:
U.S. Bank may provide an Auto Rental Collision Damage Waiver (CDW) benefit on eligible credit cards that can help cover damage to or theft of a rental vehicle, up to the vehicle's actual cash value, subject to the card's terms and conditions.
 
To be eligible for coverage, the cardholder must:
Pay for the entire rental transaction using an eligible U.S. Bank credit card. 
Decline the rental company's Collision Damage Waiver (CDW), Loss Damage Waiver (LDW), or similar optional coverage. 
Be listed as the primary renter on the rental agreement.
Please note that coverage, exclusions, and benefit limits vary by card type. Customers should review their card's Guide to Benefits or contact the card issuer directly to confirm eligibility and coverage details before relying on this benefit. 
 
 
This is just for everyone's awareness and it's always best to advise the customer that we are not in a position to discuss or determine eligibility for this benefit. We recommend that they contact CMS


• https://www.usbank.com/dam/documents/pdf/credit-cards/guide-to-benefits/Business_Visa_Sig_GTB.pdf

• https://www.usbankcanada.com/dam/documents/US-Bank-CLDI-Insurance-Certificate-English-Aug-2021.pdf

• https://cardright.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/USB_Altitude_Reserve_Visa_GTB.pdf

websitePages.name: Priority Pass Lounges
websitePages.text:
Per Quality "QA" we are not allowed to Transfer member directly to Priority Pass.
• we can only provide the # 1800-352-2834 
• or if Pax wanted to be transferred transfer Pax to CMS then ask CMS to transfer them.


Guide **How to Access Priority Pass Lounges (U.S. Bank Altitude Connect)**

•1) Register your Priority Pass account
Open this registration link: https://prioritypass.com/usbankaltitudeconnect
Follow the on-screen prompts.
Enter the required card information and personal information.
Create your User ID and Password.

Important: Save these details (you’ll need them to log in later).

•2) Log in after registration
After your account is successfully created.
Go back and log in again using the User ID and Password you created.

•3) Get your unique digital membership card
Once logged in, go inside your account dashboard.
You will see a unique digital Priority Pass card (your membership card).
Use this digital card to access eligible lounges.

•4) Using the lounge (quick steps)

When you arrive at the lounge:
Present your Priority Pass digital card from your account, and
Be ready with your boarding pass (and ID if requested).

For questions about lounge access, eligibility, or benefits, advise the cardmember to call:
1-800-352-2834ext to change this.
